text:
My kitchen has been entirely dependent on Blinkit and Instamart since my delivery… maybe others can, but I can’t do proper mindful grocery shopping with a small kid.. especially with no two-wheeler… 

plus, Blinkit lets me see the final cart value. So my expenses have already got controlled, structured and lesser than offline shopping.

Plus yeh sab karke, ghar aate aate hi thak jate hai. Man karta hai Swiggy kar lu. Wo kharcha alag! 😢

text: Ok I'm telling you my experience on blinkit shopping...first thing in bengal mirchi dhaniya free me nhi milti apko local se bhi kharidna hota hai...2nd thing alu ka price blinkit me kabhi kam nhi hota jab winters m local me alu ka price 6-8rupe kg hota tab bhi blinkit pe 20-30 rupe kg chlta hai...but yes recently dhaniya wagera blinkit pe bohot sasta tha 100gm 1rupe me bhi a rha tha..matlab 5 rupe ka half kg...abhi filhal bhindi parwal market me 30-40 rupe kg hai bhit blinkit me parwal 20rupe kg chal rha hai... I'm saying this because I'm a regular buyer from blinkit...alu peyaz adrak lasun ea sab hum sabji mandi se lete hai sabji market se nhi...

text:
I belong to India-2, and your perception of it is simply inaccurate. You seem to assume that people here buy everything from platforms like Amazon or Flipkart, but that’s not how things actually work on the ground.
No one is buying clothes or big-ticket items from Blinkit. Blinkit primarily serves a very specific purpose: groceries and daily essentials. That’s what people here use it for. Clothes and other non-essential products are still bought from Amazon, Flipkart, or Myntra.
This is the reality. When your understanding is shaped entirely by business-school frameworks and reports, and not by lived experience, you miss how people actually behave in the real world.
author: @Pycaret-uno

author: @CarYaar

text: Respected  , Here I am writing this to bring to your concern the latest supreme court judgement regarding the compulsion of the TET even for the teachers appointed before 2011 . My father got appointed as a teacher many months before TET exam was made a procedure at his time , but now he's being asked to take the exam and pass it within 2 years or else he would have to give up his job. My question is he is no more that young aspirant who can take the stress of competetive exam , he has family responsibilities , he has aged now , he has health issues . I am not saying this TET exam is wrong , what i want to say is just make the procedures for upcoming teachers , if he was appointed back then why is he being questioned now ?The govt talks of removing the pressure from students but what about those 1.86 lakh teachers who are now constantly under this pressure of cracking this exam at the age where they have served their duties for years and years and we all know how much teachers contribute other than tecahing ; the election duties,board duties , this recent SIR duty ,etc. I kindly urge you to talk about this issue as you will also get the support of those teachers too definitelty .🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏
author: @rajsingh-z8p

text:
Not gonna lie, this felt like the ultimate snack-hunting adventure!!😂❤ The variety was insane...from popping boba and focaccia crisps to matcha latte and kunafa croissant. I loved how every snack got a fair chance, whether it turned out to be a hidden gem or a complete disappointment!!😭🔥
Also, the reactions are what make these tasting videos so entertaining...the expressions say it all even before the rating is announced!!😅 Looking forward to Part 2 because Blinkit definitely has more viral snacks waiting to be reviewed!!😙🫂

author: @hpsingh13

text: It's also important to take into consideration that quick commerce assumes the majority don't plan their lives, their food and their essentials. Obviously labor costs for quick commerce have to start to reflect in the price as investors must stop funding losses. Additionally a quick commerce customer is not an acquired customer as it's most likely that there is minimal loyalty attached to the quick commerce. It's not a brand builder. It's just a short term immediate convenience.

author: @madhav9474

text:
One very important aspect about quick commerce that nobody is looking at is fire compliance of dark stores.

 If local municipal authorities make it compulsory for every dark store above 1500 feet or so to be fire compliant then half of the stores might have to close down and quick commerce might fall quickly. 

Most of the companies are dependant on dark stores relying on low rental because of being in slums, or dilapidated buildings or other illegal structures. 
I know of a dark store of zepto in Mumbai which is more than 3000 ft. carpet, under litigation property and a court case going on and no fire compliance.

God forbid but one huge fire in any of the dark stores with couple of deaths will create a havoc in the entire QC industry.

author: @varunkaushik9443

text:
I believe countries in Asia, Latin America, maybe Africa have a much better chance at excelling in quick-commerce compared to the West.

Sure population density is one reason.
The bigger reason is, even before quick-commerce, we were so used this notion of "out of eggs? Let me get it from the local grocery store, which is hardly 10 minutes away"
So, we already have this habit of buying in smaller baskets and getting things on a small notice.

Compare that to the US. People would generally go to a Costco or Walmart and get 2 weeks stuff at one go.
The habits are completely different!!

text: Simple math is groceries is a low margin game you cannot give hefty discounts, cashback to aquire customers, while customers enjoy these discounts sitting home delivered, there are unsold stocks as well which gets damaged and expired and companies are not liable for any settlements, all these years FMCG companies had a radar of 8-12% margins for local outlets and kiranas and ecommerce post 30-40% discounts on MRP for bunch of products, investors are simply getting fooled, Investment can only grow in emerging technologies or something which can bring a niche, Groceries are based on needs and rolling of inventories, the marginal outcome acts as a source of income all these years for Kiranas, and one more fact, the delivery POCs use two wheelers fir delivery they purchase from own pocket and expensed out of their own, what all they are gaining nothing 😂
